name: Braised Kosher-Cut Short Ribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet. Add ribs and sauté 2 minutes on each side or until browned. Remove from pan and set aside.
2. Add another 2 tablespoons oil to the skillet. Heat. Add cubed vegetables (onion, celery, carrot) and bay leaf. Sauté for 5 minutes or until browned.
3. Place sautéed vegetables and ribs in a roasting pan. Bake at 450°F for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Pour brandy into the pan and continue baking, covered, at 350°F for 1 hour and 45 minutes to 2 hours, or until meat falls away from bone easily.
5. Remove ribs from the pan and set aside. Discard cubed vegetables.
6. Strain the cooking liquid from the pan.
7. In a small skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil. Stir in arrowroot until smooth. Cook until golden brown.
8. Add strained liquid to the arrowroot mixture and stir until well blended.
9. Continue stirring until sauce is smooth and slightly thickened.
10. Strain again if desired. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil in a separate skillet. Add julienne onion and turnip, sautéing until lightly browned.
12. Place the sautéed vegetables on top of the short ribs and pour sauce over all.
13. Garnish with parsley before serving.
